reference: Hello, yes, you have a respiratory problem along with an esophageal tear, so you have surgical risks. Surgery should not be performed unless it is strongly indicated. If the cervical rib does not lead to significant symptoms, then there is no need for surgery. However, if surgery must be done, the anesthesiologist needs to be informed during the procedure, and two units of blood must be prepared.
text: Hello. Yes, you have a respiratory problem along with an esophageal tear, so you have surgical risks. Surg should not be performed unless it is strongly indicated. If the cervical rib does not lead to significant symptoms, then there is no need for surgery. However, if surgery must be done, the anesthesiologist needs to be informed during the procedure, and two units of blood must be prepared.
